content.full_text:
Fern Por-rrrcer- PRACTTcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
July 12,2005
Cannon Michael, President
San Luis Resource Conservation District
11704 W. Henry Miller Road
Dos Palos, California 93620
Lydia A. Beiswanger, Chief Clerk
Merced County Board of Supervisors
2222"M:',Street
Merced, California 9 53 40
Re
Your Joint Request for Advice
Our File No. 4-05-068
Dear Mr. Michael and Ms. Beiswanger:
This letter is in response to your joint request for advice regarding the potential
application of certain conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act")l as applied to the San Luis Resource Conservation District.
QUESTTONS
1. Is Merced County the code reviewing body for the San Luis Resource
Conservation District?
2. Is the San Luis Resource Conservation District's 18-year-old exemption, from
having to file a conflict-of-interest code, still valid?
CONCLUSIONS
1. No. Because San Luis Resource Conservation District is a multi-county
agerrcy, its code reviewing body is the Fair Political Practices Commission. Therefore,
the San Luis Resource Conservation District should not submit a conflict-of-interest code
to Merced County for its review.
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear attitle 2, sections
18109'18997, of the California Code of Regulations. All further retbrerrces to statutory "sections" rviil be
to the Govemment Code and all further references to "regulations" will be to title 2 of the California Code
of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
q+-q4


File No. A-05-068
Page2
2' Undetermined. It is the Executive Director of the Commission (and not the
Commission's Legal Division) who has the power to determine whether any puUfic
agency is exempt from having to file a conflict-of-interest code. Ther"ro.r, yl} request
has been referred to the commission's Executive Director for a response.
FACTS
ANALYSIS
Mr' Michae] i-s the Chairperson of the San Luis Resource Conservation District
("District'). Mr. Michael received correspondence from Ms. Beisw anger,the Chief
Clerk of the Merced County Board of Supervisors, dated November 12,2114,informing
the District that it had not submitted a corflict-of-interest code for 2004. That letter
indicated that the District would therefore be noted as a "Non-Responsive District.,,
Mr' Michael enclosed this notice for our review, as well as copies of correspondence
dated 1978 between the District and the Fair Political practices Commission
("Commission"), indicating that the District was determined by the Commission to have
been exempt from having to adopt a conflict-of-interest code in 197g.
With regard to the first question, Section S2}ll,subdivision (a), states that the
commission is the code reviewing body for..any local government agency with
jurisdiction in more than one county." (s"" also Regulaiion 1g750. r.l R ;locat
govefirment agenct'' 
-mears "a county, city or district of any kind injuding school
district, or any other local or regional poliiical subdivision, or any department, division,
bureau, office, board, commission or other agency of the foregoing.,, (Section S2O4l-) In
7 97 8, the commission granted an exemptiorLalr regulation r g7 5 r .
In Mr' Michael's May 9,2005 telephone conversation with the Commission, he
indicated that the District is a regional political entity which currently "or"r, t*d found
in both Merced and Fresno Counties.2
Since the District fits the definition of a local government agency with jurisdiction
in more than one county, we have determined that the"District's "oi" r"ul"*ing Uoay i.
not the Merced County Board of Supervisors, but continues to be the Commission.
Therefore, the power to require the District to either submit a conflict-of-interest code, or
to be exempt from that requirement, lies with the Commission and not with Merced
County.
With regard to the second question, since conflict-of-interest code exemptions
(for which the Commission is the conflict-of-interest code reviewing body) are grunted
and denied under the authority of the Commission's Executive Direito., #" i, tir"
Commission's Legal Division have forwarded this correspondence along to the Executive
Director for an evaluation as to whether the District's "rorrptio, is still valid.
' Mr' Michuel indicated that of the 65,00.0 acres covered by the District, approximat ely 20-30
acres of land is located in Fresno county; the rest is located in Mercld county. 
--')


File No. 4-05-068
Page 3
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (e16)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
C. Rockas
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

has been referred to the commission's Executive Director for a response.
